rows.objective: Describe the body’s basic organization and composition

ref_details.title: Skin Integrity and Pressure Injury Prevention

rows.objective: List the body systems, including basic anatomy and physiology, common diseases of the elderly with signs and symptoms, Nurse Assistant duties and observations, aging changes and complications of immobility

ref_details.title: Range of Motion and Restorative Care
Skin Integrity and Pressure Injury Prevention

assessed_module_checks: Mobility, Falls, and Workplace Safety Knowledge Check
Nutrition, Skin Integrity, Vital Signs, and Observation Knowledge Check
rows.note: Body systems / immobility complications applied in M04 (body mechanics, ROM) and M05/L03 (skin); systematic A&P not the recert-theory focus.

rows.objective: Describe changes in body systems associated with aging Refer to CDPH 276C form: *SNF/ICF providers need to complete A1 Special needs of persons with developmental and mental disorders including intellectual disability, cerebral palsy, epilepsy, Parkinson’s disease, and mental illness (2 hours); A2 Special needs of persons with Alzheimer’s disease and related dementias (2 hours); and B Introduction to anatomy and physiology (B-F minimum 1 hour). **Non-SNF/ICF providers may complete A2 Special needs of persons with Alzheimer’s disease and related dementias (2 hours), or two (2) hours of training from another module in order to meet the sixty (60) hours minimum theory training requirement and B Introduction to anatomy and physiology (B-F minimum 1 hour).
